failing_pipelines.issue: Dependency conflict: pycookiecheat requires cryptography 42.* or 43.*, but dependabot trying to update to 46.0.5
failing_pipelines.diagnosis: pycookiecheat pins cryptography to old versions (0.7.0->42.*, 0.8.0->43.*), incompatible with latest cryptography 46.0.5
failing_pipelines.recommended_action: Wait for pycookiecheat upstream to release version supporting cryptography 46.x

failing_pipelines.issue: Security update not possible - micromatch update blocked by dependency tree
failing_pipelines.diagnosis: Multiple existing PRs for axios, webpack, babel/runtime, form-data create complex dependency graph preventing micromatch update
failing_pipelines.recommended_action: Merge or close conflicting PRs first, then retry micromatch update

failing_pipelines.issue: Pipfile resolution failure - dependency conflict with cryptography
failing_pipelines.diagnosis: Similar to redhat-ai-workflow, pycookiecheat dependency blocks cryptography update
ai-responder.py expects OPENAI_API_KEY but secret distributed is AI_API_KEY
failing_pipelines.recommended_action: Non-critical - ai-responder.py is being superseded by this swarm system. Can fix by renaming secret or updating ai-responder.py

next_iteration_recommendations: Monitor Chuckbot - newly merged PR #40 should trigger successful CI run
Dependency conflicts (redhat-ai-workflow, aa-dev-prod-watcher, jira-creator) require upstream package updates - no immediate action possible

AI responder failures (ollama-proxy, sodarr-chrome-plugin, docker-media-center) are due to env var naming - can be fixed by updating ai-responder.yml to use AI_API_KEY instead of OPENAI_API_KEY, or by distributing OPENAI_API_KEY secret
All 14 open issues have received responses - no triage work needed
System remains in steady state - all automatically fixable issues resolved
